mnemonics: Dense Clusters: Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN) finds dense regions separated by areas of lower density, like finding crowded islands in a sparse sea.
keyPoints: DBSCAN does not require pre-specifying the number of clusters (k).
It can find arbitrarily shaped clusters, not just spherical ones like k-means.
It identifies outliers as points that do not belong to any dense region (noise).
commonMistakes: Improperly tuning the eps (neighborhood radius) and minPts (minimum points) parameters, which can lead to all points being labeled as a single cluster or all as noise.
Assuming the algorithm will perform well on data with clusters of widely varying densities, which is a weakness of standard DBSCAN.
